old_claim.note: Telescope provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The scoped catalog is anchored to printed publication of Tycho's shorter 777-star catalog in 1602, so print publication is an enabling distribution mechanism.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract: Tycho completed a 1004-star catalog in 1598, circulated it in manuscript, edited a 777-star version printed in 1602, and Kepler edited the full version in 1627.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source anchors the scoped star-catalog node to printed publication of Tycho's 777-star catalog in 1602.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the node is a published observatory star catalog rather than a telescope observation program, print publication is the relevant distribution dependency and telescope is outside the causal chain.

ontology_before: Observatory Star Catalogs directly depended on telescope, implying a telescopic star-catalog origin.
ontology_after: Tycho Brahe Observatory Star Catalogs depend on the Uraniborg measurement program and print publication, without a direct telescope prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: observatory_star_catalogs now depends on printing_press and no longer directly depends on telescope.
would_reject_if: The node were broadened to later telescope-based star catalogs such as Flamsteed.
A source showed Tycho's 1602 star catalog depended on telescopic observations.
The node were narrowed to manuscript-only catalog circulation rather than printed publication.
short_reason: Tycho's 1602 star catalog was printed from pre-telescopic observatory data.
